services.reason: The evaluator runs inside this service, so self-probing would not provide independent availability evidence; no external objective is approved.
A ten-minute health probe exists, but no availability target has been owner-approved; its counters remain observational until a target is deliberately set.
Liveness alone would not prove successful scheduled delivery; no end-to-end digest delivery objective or journey is approved.
The Worker has a health route, but the estate does not yet probe it or have an approved target; DORA correctness is not inferred from liveness.
The existing probe evaluates quota threshold state as well as reachability, so its counters are not a pure availability indicator and no objective is approved.
Reachability is probed and sleeping local AI is intentionally healthy, but no edge availability target has been owner-approved.
Sentinel silence contributes to the machine verdict, but there is no independent sentinel probe; a direct objective would reuse its own derived evidence.
No dedicated uptime probe or approved availability target exists; keep the service unmeasured until independent evidence is added.
